name: Kan Kusakabe
affiliation:
Hokkaido University 

 PhD of Computer Science
introduction: Hi, I'm Kan Kusakabe, a second‑year PhD student in Information Science at Hokkaido University and an HCI researcher. My work centers on interactive mobile interfaces (accessory‑style designs, advanced sensing, and gesture input). By combining machine learning with rapid prototyping and 3D printing, I create solutions that seamlessly blend digital and physical experiences. I'm always eager to discuss research collaborations, internships, or career opportunities. Thanks for visiting my portfolio, and feel free to reach out!
news: Invited Talk at [Public Lecture / Yokohama City MICE Next-Generation Development Initiative: CHI 2025 Symposium](https://sigchi.jp/symposium/chi2025_symposium/) (Apr 26, 2025)

Oral presentation at CHI 2025 titled ''[Understanding Usability of VR Pointing Methods with a Handheld-style HMD for Onsite Exhibitions](https://kankusakabe.github.io/Portfolio/ja/works/handheld-styleHMD)'' (Apr 30, 2025)
